task.title: Manually setting the wireless regulatory domain

li.p: The driver of the wifi device supports changing the regulatory domain.

context.p: On RHEL, a udev rule executes the setregdomain utility to set the wireless regulatory domain. The utility then provides this information to the kernel.

By default, setregdomain attempts to determine the country code automatically. If this fails, the wireless regulatory domain setting might be wrong. To work around this problem, you can manually set the country code.

note.p: Manually setting the regulatory domain disables the automatic detection. Therefore, if you later use the computer in a different country, the previously configured setting might no longer be correct. In this case, remove the /etc/sysconfig/regdomain file to switch back to automatic detection or use this procedure to manually update the regulatory domain setting again.

step.cmd: Optional: Display the current regulatory domain settings:

info.codeblock: # iw reg get global country US: DFS-FCC ...

step.cmd: Create the /etc/sysconfig/regdomain file with the following content:

info.p: Set the COUNTRY variable to an ISO 3166-1 alpha2 country code, such as DE for Germany or US for the United States of America.

li.codeblock: # iw reg get global country DE: DFS-ETSI ...
